options.description: output usage information. Can be used with individual commands too

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: add a native platform project to your app

subcommands.name: copy
subcommands.description: copy the web app build and Capacitor configuration file into the native platform project. Run this each time you make changes to your web app or change a config

subcommands.name: ls
subcommands.description: list all installed Cordova and Capacitor plugins

subcommands.name: open
subcommands.description: opens the native project workspace in the specified native IDE (Xcode for iOS, Android Studio for Android)

subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: opens the native project workspace in the specified native IDE (Xcode for iOS, Android Studio for Android)

options.description: print a list of target devices available to the given platform
run on a specific target device

subcommands.name: sync
subcommands.description: this command runs copy and then update

options.description: podfile.lock won't be deleted and pod install will use --deployment option
after syncing, all JS source maps will be inlined allowing for debugging an Android Web View in Chromium based browsers

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: updates the native plugins and dependencies referenced in package.json

options.description: podfile.lock won't be deleted and pod install will use --deployment option
